title: Add Modal single-node 8xH100 launcher
author: Muhtasham
body:
## Summary

Adds a small single-node Modal launcher for the public Parameter Golf baseline stack so experiments can run on `8xH100` with persistent Modal Volumes and a minimal setup path.

## What changed

- add `modal_train.py` as a single-node Modal launcher for the public `torch 2.9.1 + cu128` stack
- use explicit file mounts instead of mounting the full repo
- persist datasets, tokenizers, Hugging Face cache, and run outputs in dedicated Modal Volumes
- support running either `train_gpt.py` or another explicitly requested script via `--script`
- run distributed training in-process with `torch.distributed.run` rather than shelling out to `torchrun`
- add a README section covering `modal setup`, `modal run`, `--skip-dataset`, and `--extra-env`
- ignore local `.env` files in `.gitignore`

## Why

The repo docs point people to Runpod for the canonical evaluation environment, but Modal is still useful for fast iteration:

- startup is quick
- volumes make repeated dataset/tokenizer reuse cheap
- single-node `8xH100` runs are easy to launch from one command

This keeps the launcher out of `train_gpt.py` itself and keeps the change infra-only.

## Validation

- `python3 -m py_compile modal_train.py`
- Modal smoke run on `H100!:8`
- full public-baseline-style Modal runs using `train_gpt.py`

Representative full-run observations on Modal:

- training loop respects the ~10 minute wallclock cap (`train_time` ~599s inside trainer logs)
- public stack reproduces the expected single-node baseline behavior well enough for iteration
- exact Modal perf still differs from the archived/OpenAI baseline, so this is positioned as an iteration launcher rather than a claim of canonical leaderboard parity

## Notes

- this is intentionally single-node only; it does not add multi-node `@clustered` support
- this keeps `train_gpt.py` unchanged and follows the repo guidance that core scripts stay simple while heavier experimentation lives outside them
